mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero or declining over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of rising rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or mean forward returns in low/falling-rate months are not significantly higher than in rising-rate months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a threshold indicating a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions, forward-looking earnings expectations deteriorate and equity risk premia rise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the average triggered-period return is not statistically different from the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with an accommodative federal funds rate signals robust consumer demand financed under cheap credit conditions, jointly producing a short-term positive equity reaction over the next week that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% while FEDFUNDS below 1%
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-FEDFUNDS interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either univariate signal (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10) across a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding real output capacity and corporate earnings power, translating into persistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Correlation between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 20d SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, continued strong payroll growth signals labor market tightness that raises wage-cost pressure and inflation expectations, causing markets to price in future monetary tightening and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; the same payroll growth in early-cycle recovery produces positive returns instead.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k while UNRATE below 4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Direction of the PAYEMS-return relationship does not flip sign between early-cycle (UNRATE > 6%) and late-cycle (UNRATE < 4.5%) subsamples, or the late-cycle negative effect is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets anticipate broader demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following triggered HOUST declines are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or effect is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in CPI inflation above trend, defined as a month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4%, raises expectations of imminent monetary tightening, causing a short-term negative equity reaction over the following week as discount rates are repriced higher.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return following triggered CPI surprises is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) across a 5-year walk-forward sample, or is positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and contraction in retail sales indicates a synchronized demand-labor downturn that amplifies recession risk beyond what either indicator implies alone, producing sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while RSAFS month-over-month change is negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-RSAFS interaction signal fails to outperform either univariate signal in out-of-sample rank IC, or triggered episodes show positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases.
